_comment: Node 7 v2 - Phase 2 Flux migration workflow (design-locked 2026-04-26; 2a shipped 2026-04-26, 2b 2026-04-27, 2c 2026-04-27, 2d-prep 2026-04-27, 2d-fixup 2026-04-27, Phase 2-revision 2026-04-28). One file handles BOTH dwpose and lineart-fallback routes via per-detection swaps applied by orchestrate.py: node 51 class_type (DWPreprocessor for humans / LineArtPreprocessor for non-humans) and node 61 type ('openpose' / 'lineart'). Node IDs are locked across all Phase 2 phases (2a-2g); see custom_nodes/node_07_pose_refiner/README.md for the full table. Re-exporting from ComfyUI's GUI must preserve these IDs OR update orchestrate.py's NODE_FLUX_* constants in the same commit. Phase 2c flipped node 80 from EmptySD3LatentImage (txt2img) to VAEEncode (img2img) of the rough crop, with KSampler denoise=0.55 per locked decision #5; --workflow default also flipped from 'v1' to 'v2' (Phase 1 path stays callable via --workflow=v1 for the deprecation window). Phase 2b wired the XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 via nodes 22 LoadFluxIPAdapter, 23 LoadImage for reference color crop, 24 ApplyFluxIPAdapter; KSampler's model input goes through node 24. Phase 2d-fixup (2026-04-27, post-live-pod debug) corrected the wire-protocol class_type strings (NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS keys, not display names) + IP-Adapter weight destination path. Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28) made TWO changes: (1) node 50's LoadImage now receives a per-character bbox crop produced by orchestrate.py's _prepare_rough_bbox_crop (NOT the whole-frame keypose Phase 2c briefly fed it); the bbox crop becomes the source for both the pose preprocessor (node 51) and VAEEncode (node 80), aligning v2 with Phase 1 locked decision #5 (per-character generation). (2) node 20's strength_model + strength_clip dropped from 0.75 to 0.0 — the generic Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 LoRA biases toward color, conflicting with Part 1's BnW line-art deliverable, so it's bypassed via zero strength while still loaded (LoraLoader can't be skipped without rewiring nodes 30 + 24). Locked decision #2 production strength of 0.75 survives intact for the custom TMKOC v1 LoRA shipping in Phase 2d-run (orchestrate.py STYLE_LORA_STRENGTHS table picks the right strength per --style-lora). Prompt strings on nodes 30 + 31 are re-asserted by orchestrate.py at parameterize time and ask for BnW line art (positive) + reject color/fill/shading/scene (negative). Phase 2e will wire a chained second LoraLoader at node 21 for per-character LoRAs (slots between node 20 and node 24 without renaming any existing ID).

20._comment: Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28): strength_model + strength_clip dropped from 0.75 to 0.0. The generic Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 LoRA biases toward color, conflicting with Part 1's BnW line-art deliverable. The LoRA still loads (LoraLoader can't be skipped without rewiring nodes 30 + 24) but contributes nothing at strength 0. orchestrate.py's STYLE_LORA_STRENGTHS picks the right strength per --style-lora flag at parameterize time: flat_cartoon_v12 → 0.0 (bypass), tmkoc_v1 → 0.75 (locked decision #2 production value, applied once Phase 2d-run ships the custom-trained TMKOC line-art LoRA). The lora_name field is also parameterized per --style-lora.

22._comment: XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 loader. class_type is the INTERNAL NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS key from x-flux-comfyui/nodes.py — 'LoadFluxIPAdapter' (no spaces, no typo). The 'IPAdatpter' typo is in upstream's NODE_DISPLAY_NAME_MAPPINGS (the GUI menu label only) — it does NOT appear in class_type. The input field name 'ipadatper' IS typo'd verbatim (that's how XLabs registered the field name in INPUT_TYPES) so we keep it. Reads 'ipadatper' file from models/xlabs/ipadapters/ (the path x-flux-comfyui's folder_paths registration looks at — NOT models/ipadapter-flux/ which is a separate convention). Pairs with CLIP-L (openai/clip-vit-large-patch14) placed at models/clip_vision/clip-vit-large-patch14.safetensors via runpod_setup.sh.

23._comment: Reference COLOR crop from Node 6E (NOT the DoG line-art crop). Locked decision #4: IP-Adapter expects a textured/colored image for its identity embedding. Orchestrator parameterizes 'image' per detection.

24._comment: Wraps the LoRA-conditioned Flux UNET with IP-Adapter cross-attention to the reference color crop. ip_scale = 0.8 per locked decision #6 (XLabs default is 0.93; 0.8 trades a bit of identity strength for slightly looser pose adherence — better for varied keyposes across a shot).

50._comment: Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28): the 'image' input is parameterized to a PER-CHARACTER bbox crop produced by orchestrate.py's _prepare_rough_bbox_crop (NOT the whole-frame keypose Phase 2c briefly fed). The crop is (Node 5 bbox + 20% margin) clamped to image bounds and resized to a Flux-compatible canvas (multiples of 16, longest edge ≤ 768). Feeds both the pose preprocessor (node 51) and VAEEncode (node 80) so the entire downstream Flux stack operates on character-only pixels. Aligns v2 with Phase 1 locked decision #5 (per-character generation, NOT whole-frame inpaint). Node 8's compositor places the refined output back at the bbox position via feet-pinned scaling.

80._comment: Phase 2c: img2img mode. VAEEncode the rough into a Flux latent so KSampler with denoise=0.55 preserves rough composition (positions, scale, silhouette) while regenerating line quality + character identity (locked decision #5). Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28): the 'pixels' source from node 50 is now a per-character bbox crop (NOT the whole-frame keypose Phase 2c fed); see node 50's _comment. The bbox crop is sized to multiples of 16 (Flux requirement) by _prepare_rough_bbox_crop in orchestrate.py — typically 768×768 or smaller depending on aspect ratio of the bbox + margin. Output dimensions match the bbox crop's dims; Node 8's compositor doesn't care about exact output dims since it places refined PNGs at bbox positions per Node 8 locked decision #1.

90._comment: model input wired from node 24 (IP-Adapter wrapped model) since Phase 2b. In Phase 2a this was wired directly from node 20 (style LoRA output). When Phase 2e adds the chained character LoraLoader at node 21, the chain becomes 10 -> 20 -> 21 -> 24 -> 90 — node 24 still feeds KSampler. Phase 2c: denoise dropped from 1.0 (txt2img) to 0.55 (img2img) per locked decision #5 — preserves rough composition while regenerating line quality + character identity.
